item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Loyalty Rewards & How to Claim Them
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 has raced onto the scene and offers a stacked roster of cars to collect, and for those with experience in the franchise, loyalty rewards are available to offer an immediate boost to your collection.

Over 600 officially licensed cars are available to own in Forza Horizon 6, with more set to arrive in post-launch content, including DLCs, and you won’t need to splash out to obtain all of them, as some are distributed for free.

If you’re looking to maximise your car collection in Forza Horizon 6 with loyalty rewards, Insider Gaming has all the details you need.

All Forza Horizon 6 Loyalty Rewards

There is a maximum of six loyalty rewards available in Forza Horizon 6, all of which are obtained through having save data on previous games in the Forza franchise.

Aside from the Chevrolet Corvette E-Ray, which is awarded for having played Forza Motorsport, all of the reward cards are earned from save data in the five previous Horizon games, stretching back to the initial title.

  • More Insider Gaming: Forza Horizon 6 Review

Each of the cars should look familiar to players of the franchise, as they are all the title cars featured on the artwork of their respective games.

The table below shows all of the available loyalty rewards in Forza Horizon 6, and the corresponding titles you need to have in your collection.

How to Claim Loyalty Rewards in Forza Horizon 6

Forza Horizon 6 loyalty rewards are automatically redeemed inside the game, and don’t need to manually be activated, but there is a small delay in distribution.

In order to receive the loyalty rewards, you must begin your Forza Horizon 6 journey and complete the introductory stages of the game when you first arrive in Japan.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Forza Horizon 6 on Xbox Game Pass?

Once you have completed the prologue and unlocked your first house and garage, each of the loyalty reward cars will be added to your roster of vehicles and can be selected to drive at any time.

You won’t be able to use them in race events immediately, however, as you’ll need to progress through the various wristbands to unlock races in their respective car classes, but you can use them in exploration and for open-world events like Danger Signs and Drift Zones.

How many of the loyalty rewards are you getting in Forza Horizon 6? Share your rewards in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Forza Horizon 6, take a look at all of the available multiplayer modes, and check out the full list of achievements.

item.title: Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth series shipments and digital sales top three million
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS Vita
  • PS4
  • Switch

Titles currently available at a deep discount.

Total worldwide shipments and digital sales for Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth and Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker’s Memory have surpassed three million units, Bandai Namco announced (2).

Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth first launched for PS Vita in Japan in March 2015, followed by PlayStation 4 and PS Vita in North America and Europe in February 2016, PlayStation 4 in Japan in December 2017, and Switch and PC via Steam worldwide in October 2019.

Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker’s Memory launched for PlayStation 4 and PS Vita in December 2017 in Japan and January 2018 worldwide, followed by Switch and PC via Steam worldwide in October 2019.

The series was last reported to have surpassed 1.5 million units in October 2020.

To celebrate the milestone, both titles are available at a deep discount price for a limited time.

item.title: Xbox is reportedly creating a new Game Pass tier to capture the ever growing Chinese gaming market with
item.content:encoded:
According to a new report, and hints from new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma, Microsoft is rolling out a new tier of Game Pass specifically aimed at Chinese gamers.

This information, coming from Windows Central, claims that "Project Saluki" is that new tier, aimed at appealing to gamers present in the Chinese market. It'll do so by being tailored by "China's unique regulatory environment and gamer preferences."

This would come at a time when the Chinese market has proven exceptionally powerful for the video game industry. Big releases from the region have proven that developers there are more than capable at creating worldwide hits, such as Black Myth Wukong which shot past 10m sales across all platforms in short order.

An increasing number of Steam users are also Chinese. A March 2025 Steam user survey found that those using simplified Chinese on the platform increased by 20.88 percent, reaching roughly half of all users.

It would also come at a time when Xbox is sorely in need of an ace or two up its sleeve. While it has gained a new CEO recently in Sharma, the Xbox Series X/S has certainly struggled, suffering the worst year on record in the UK last year. Sharma has pushed forward some bold initiatives to help combat this, including a revamp of the leadership team and a new Discord x Game Pass partnership.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/xbox-new-game-pass-tier-china-project-saluki

item.title: NAVI to Face Vitality in IEM Atlanta Quarter-Final after Legacy Loss
item.content:encoded:
The playoff bracket has been set for IEM Atlanta 2026 after a dramatic final day of groups that saw NAVI fall to Legacy to set up another contest with Vitality.

The final day of groups also saw Astralis, FUT, Liquid, and FaZe amongst the teams to leave the competition, leaving one side of the bracket wide open in Atlanta.

NAVI and Vitality set for Quarter-Final clash

After Vitality’s upset loss to BetBoom, they once again looked vulnerable against FaZe on Nuke before converting the map in overtime, going on to win the series 2-0 before brushing B8 aside to qualify for the playoffs.

Seemingly not content to let Vitality face the Quarter-Finals without them, NAVI went on to lose 2-1 to Legacy after a strong showing from ex-FURIA IGL Andrei ‘arT’ Piovezan on Inferno, and stand out performances from Bruno ‘latto’ Rebelatto and Eduardo ‘dumau’ Wolkmer on Dust 2, despite a strong series from Ihor ‘w0nderful’ Zhdanov.

Going into the Quarter-Finals, a loss to Vitality would make it four events in a row where NAVI are eliminated by Vitality after Grand Final losses at BLAST Open Rotterdam and BLAST Rivals Fort Worth were bisected by a Quarter-Final loss at IEM Rio 2026. All without a single map win.

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth saw NAVI come closest to winning a map, after an 11-1 CT-side on Nuke was followed by an infamous collapse on their own T-side.

Whether that particular map was a sign that NAVI can put Vitality in danger, or whether it has fully drained NAVI of any mental ability to fight Vitality, will be answered in the Quarter-Final.

With NAVI faltering against Legacy, suffering illness within their camp through Mihai ‘iM’ Ivan and Drin Shaqiri, it doesn’t seem like the moment for NAVI to step up.

The IEM Atlanta 2026 Playoff Bracket

  • NAVI vs Vitality
  • paiN vs GamerLegion
  • BetBoom vs NAVI/Vitality winner
  • Legacy vs paiN/GamerLegion winner

The event will culminate in a Best of 5 Grand Final at the Georgia World Congress Center.

A win for paiN against GamerLegion would guarantee Brazilian representation in the Grand Final after paiN’s victories over FaZe and FUT guaranteed them a place in the playoffs. Legacy awaits the winner after victories against M80, Astralis, and NAVI have put the team in red-hot form at Atlanta.

As for BetBoom, their victory over Vitality will give them plenty of confidence for a potential rematch, and that rematch will be Best of 3, potentially helping the Russian roster.

However, Vitality have, of course, shown more dominant form in the playoffs than in the groups of late, remaining strong favourites for the trophy despite the Quarter-Final start.

Do you think NAVI is getting closer or further away from Vitality? You can discuss that on our Discord!
Also see Insider Gaming’s full aleksib interview here.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

item.title: Assassin's Creed Hexe Image Allegedly Leaks, Alongside Word of a Major Returning Character
item.content:encoded:
Another day, another set of Assassin's Creed leaks — this time for the series' upcoming witchcraft-inspired blockbuster Assassin's Creed Hexe, which is expected to launch at some point in 2027.

The latest details come from persistent Ubisoft leaker j0nathan, who claims to have published the first screenshot taken from the game. The image shows a hooded Assassin figure perched on a branch in a dark and creepy forest, their Hidden Blade ready to strike.

j0nathan also discussed a series of purported Hexe plot details that have made their way around Assassin's Creed fan sites and social media this week: that the game's main hero is a woman named Anika, who has an older Assassin mentor named Wolfgang.

ASSASSIN’S CREED HEXE’den ilk ekran görüntüsü sızdırıldı! 👀

Solda sızdırılan görüntüyü, sağda ise yapay zekâ yardımıyla netleştirilmiş versiyonunu görüyorsunuz.

(@xj0nathan) pic.twitter.com/auZGT4bBkc

— IGN Türkiye (@IGN_Turkey) May 14, 2026

And then there's the most spoiler-y element of the leak — one we'll place behind a warning should you not wish to know this information ahead of time.

Warning! Potential spoilers for Assassin's Creed Hexe follow:

Most surprising of all is word that iconic Assassin hero Ezio Auditore will return within Hexe in some capacity. The exact nature of how this will take place doesn't sound clear, but j0nathan has suggested Ezio will not physically be present, running around in Hexe's European forests.

Instead, the famed Assassin's Creed Brotherhood protagonist will make his presence felt via some sort of Isu recording — a memory disc or similar technology (perhaps akin to the memory discs Ezio used to discover more about Altaïr in Assassin's Creed Revelations).

The Complete Assassin's Creed Timeline

Of course, it's worth caveating all of this with the fact that games in development are subject to change (and indeed, Hexe has reportedly gone through changes recently to rework its more magical elements). None of this information comes officially from Ubisoft, either, though the leaker has a lengthy track record.

Earlier this month, j0nathan sparked an official response from Ubisoft when he shared a work-in-progress image of the upcoming Assassin's Creed Invictus multiplayer spinoff. Ubisoft claimed the image had been altered using AI — something j0nathan later admitted, before publishing the original, unedited screenshot instead.

IGN has contacted Ubisoft for more.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

item.title: Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls' 10-Hour Story Mode Will Feature Gameplay After All
item.content:encoded:
Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls developer Arc System Works recently revealed that the fighting game will have an Episode Mode — a story mode aimed at casual players — told in an interactive comic book style. Various outlets originally reported that this Episode Mode wouldn't have any actual gameplay. But recent updates have confirmed that this is not the case.

In an interview with Japanese news site 4Gamer, producer Takeshi Yamanaka and director/lead battle designer Kazuto ‘Pachi’ Sekine revealed more about the game’s story mode. However, 4Gamer’s initial publication suggested that Episode Mode would only contain a Guilty Gear Strive-style cutscene-based story mode with no gameplay. But an update to the interview, added on May 13, clarified that there will indeed be 4v4 bouts to enjoy within the story mode.

“In Episode Mode, playable battles are incorporated throughout,” clarified Yamanaka. “Battles occur that tie into the story, while the design allows everyone to enjoy it casually, just like reading a comic book. This allows the story to be experienced in a more interactive way.”

Episode mode will feature around 10 hours of content and gameplay in total, with variations in the story playing out depending on which of the five default teams you pick. The story will build up to a confrontation against Marvel Tokon’s brand-new, mysterious villains: The Champion and his henchwoman Promoter. Teams of four will battle it out in order to face The Champion in the final battle of the game’s Episode Mode, and prevent him from destroying Earth (as detailed in the PlayStation blog post earlier this month).

The comic book-style cutscenes will be animated and voiced in 10 languages, with players flipping through panels and scenes. The interactive comic book will make use of the PS5 DualSense's haptic feedback, too. “The default teams revealed by the official channels have been put together with the Episode Mode storyline in mind,” noted Yamanaka, however those who want more freedom can make their own teams in the game’s other modes.

Marvel Tokon’s 4v4 tag team battle system is based around one of the chosen characters in each team being the leader. "We want players to be able to freely select four characters that they like, so we’ve included a 'Team Generation' feature that automatically generates a team name based on the characters chosen," explained Yamanaka. The source words used for this name generation system were picked by the dev team, and are selected based on the characteristics of your team leader and the other members. Players are also free to name their teams themselves.

The 10 Best Fighting Games

Yamanaka also revealed that Marvel urged the team to incorporate Arc System Works’ art style into the character designs, giving the superheroes an anime-style look. “This is why one of our themes for this game is 'from Japan to the world' — the idea of drawing Marvel characters in Arc System Works' unique style and sending them out into the world," said Sekine.

Yamanaka noted that in-depth discussions were held between Sony, Marvel, and Arc System Works on the character roster, but that the developers were given freedom by Marvel to interpret guidelines for each character how they saw fit, sending their work to Marvel for ultimate approval. Sekine also hinted that fans should keep an eye out for some interesting alternative costumes.

Yamanaka and Sekine were keen to emphasize that Marvel Tokon is not just for hardcore Marvel fans but also can be enjoyed by casual players and newcomers to the superhero franchise. "The Episode Mode includes glossaries and character bios — we designed the game so even Marvel newcomers can understand the world, the characters and the relationships between them. I'd be happy if Marvel Tokon served as a gateway for people to discover the movies and comics as well."

Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls is due out on PlayStation 5 and PC on August 6, 2026.

Verity Townsend is a Japan-based freelance writer who previously served as editor, contributor and translator for the game news site Automaton West. She has also written about Japanese culture and movies for various publications.
